benchmark: vyre-frontend-c real_corpus Criterion batch syntax parser

backend.cpu_execution: tree-sitter baseline and oracle/test infrastructure only

unsafe_batch_chunking.reason: measured real-corpus lexer throughput improves when unsafe concatenated C batches stay near 112 KiB instead of one 629 KiB conservative lexer batch

commands: env RUST_MIN_STACK=67108864 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p vyre-frontend-c --test parse_syntax_bytes -- --nocapture
env RUST_MIN_STACK=67108864 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p vyre-frontend-c -- --nocapture
env RUST_MIN_STACK=67108864 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 VYRE_BACKEND=cuda cargo bench -p vyre-frontend-c --bench real_corpus -- --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --sample-size 10

test_results.gnu_escape_e2e: included in full package and focused run; passed

notes: This is a fair cold tree-sitter batch comparison from the same Criterion bench binary.
This is not a 10x result; it is the current audited real-corpus win after chunked CUDA raw syntax batching.
The larger release goal remains incomplete: clang-parity semantic analysis and full-kernel 10x speed targets are not yet proven.
